candidate.title: Distributed training and orchestration scheduling broadening

local_checks.raw_manifest_scan: Targeted scans over personal-wiki/domains/ai_infra/raw found reusable NCCL technical-blog captures for dynamic communicators and fault-tolerant collectives, existing Ray Data source notes, Kubernetes/Volcano/Kueue closed issue summaries and indexes, Kueue quota governance source notes, compute accelerator Gaudi/AMD discovery captures, and SGLang issue/PR titles mentioning FSDP, Megatron, Slurm, AMD GPU, and Gaudi. No existing raw/links notes before this task directly covered PyTorch FSDP/DCP/torchrun, DeepSpeed ZeRO/checkpointing, Ray Train fault tolerance, KubeRay orchestration, Slurm GPU/GRES scheduling, NVIDIA/AMD GPU operators, Kubernetes device plugins, or Kubeflow Trainer.
local_checks.wiki_search: Targeted scans over wiki/index.md, coverage-map.json, loop-state.json, ingest.md, NCCL pages, Inference Runtime Infrastructure, Data RAG Pipeline Infrastructure, Security Governance Cost Infrastructure, and Kubernetes/Volcano/Kueue pages found no dedicated Distributed Training Infrastructure or Orchestration Scheduling Infrastructure page before this task. Existing NCCL pages covered communication, observability, reliability, and fabric behavior; existing Kubernetes/Volcano/Kueue pages covered closed issue corpora and quota governance, not Ray, Slurm, device-plugin, GPU-operator, or training-controller boundaries.
local_checks.domain_index_scan: personal-wiki/domains/ai_infra/wiki/index.md already linked NCCL, SGLang, Kubernetes, Volcano, Kueue, NCCL Technical Blog Network Observability, Security Governance Cost Infrastructure, Inference Runtime Infrastructure, and Data RAG Pipeline Infrastructure. New reference pages are non-duplicative because one organizes training framework lifecycle/checkpoint/elastic mechanics beyond NCCL, and the other organizes scheduler/device/operator/controller boundaries beyond Kubernetes, Volcano, and Kueue issue corpora.

gap_reason: coverage-map.json marked training-distributed partial for framework, checkpointing, and elastic training evidence beyond NCCL, and marked orchestration-scheduling partial for Ray, Slurm-on-Kubernetes or HPC scheduling, device plugins, GPU operators, and accelerator quota/operator boundaries. Local evidence supplied useful duplicate boundaries and operational leads but did not provide source-backed curated pages for the two remaining backlog items.

selected_source_candidates.coverage: Existing NCCL communication, RAS, dynamic communicator, and fabric evidence reused as a duplicate boundary rather than as complete training lifecycle evidence.
Existing Kubernetes, Volcano, and Kueue closed issue corpora reused as operational scheduling evidence with comment-completeness caveats.
Existing source note reused for ResourceQuota, ClusterQueue, ResourceFlavor, cohort, and accelerator quota/admission boundaries.
PyTorch distributed process groups, FSDP, distributed checkpointing, torchrun, and elastic/restart behavior.
DeepSpeed ZeRO sharding, checkpoint conversion, Universal Checkpoints, CPU/NVMe offload, and Megatron-LM project boundary evidence.
Ray Train checkpoint/fault tolerance plus KubeRay RayCluster, RayJob, RayService, scheduling integration, autoscaling, and GPU execution boundaries.
Slurm GPU/GRES scheduling, job allocation, job-step allocation, and HPC scheduler boundaries.
Kubernetes device plugins, NVIDIA device plugin, NVIDIA GPU Operator, and AMD GPU Operator device/resource/node lifecycle boundaries.
Kubeflow Trainer, TrainJob lifecycle, framework-specific Kubernetes training job guides, and scheduling integrations.

duplicate_boundaries.nccl_communication: NCCL release, issue, and blog evidence remains communication/fabric coverage. It is cited only to distinguish collective-level fault tolerance from framework-level checkpoint/restart semantics.
duplicate_boundaries.kubernetes_volcano_kueue_issue_corpora: Existing issue corpora remain operational evidence for Kubernetes-native scheduling, but they do not replace Ray/KubeRay, Slurm/GRES, device-plugin, GPU-operator, or Kubeflow Trainer design sources.
duplicate_boundaries.security_governance_cost: Existing Kueue quota and ResourceQuota coverage is reused for accelerator admission boundaries; cost, tenant isolation, and capacity planning stay in Security Governance Cost Infrastructure.
duplicate_boundaries.inference_runtime: SGLang/vLLM/Megatron/FSDP/Slurm issue titles and inference runtime pages are treated as discovery leads or runtime signals, not as primary training framework or scheduling design evidence.
duplicate_boundaries.hardware_accelerator: Compute accelerator Gaudi and AMD pages support hardware inventory and SKU boundaries, not Kubernetes operator behavior unless paired with device-plugin or GPU-operator docs.

git_status_before_task: git status --short returned no changed paths before edits.

shell_probe_blocked_or_auth_sources.evidence: curl -L --max-time 12 -I failed with DNS resolution error in the restricted exec environment; browser tool source opening succeeded for official documentation pages.
